title: My First Job Interview
summary: Past simple + present perfect — describing a memorable experience.
text:
I have had many job interviews in my life, but I will never forget the first one. It was five years ago, just after I finished university. I applied for a position at a small marketing company.

The day of the interview, I was very nervous. I woke up early and put on my best suit. When I arrived at the office, I noticed I had forgotten my CV at home. I panicked, but the receptionist was kind. She let me print another copy.

The interview lasted thirty minutes. The manager asked me about my studies and my hobbies. I tried to sound confident, although my hands were shaking. At one point, I made a joke and the manager laughed. I think that helped me.

One week later, they called me. I had got the job! I worked there for three years. Looking back, I have learned that being prepared is important, but being human is what really matters.

questions.q: How long ago was the first interview?
How long did the interview last?
What helped the narrator in the interview?
What lesson does the narrator share at the end?
